objective: Add the org-level governance SSOT consumer adoption drift report, wire it into the weekly overlord sweep, and close out epic #452 with issue-backed residual status.
scope_boundary: Add a non-mutating cross-repo consumer status reporter.
Wire the reporter into the weekly overlord sweep report.
Record org-level adoption status, residual drift, validation, and Stage 6 closeout evidence.
Update governance roadmap surfaces with issue #459 and epic #452 status.
out_of_scope: Changing repository GitHub settings or rulesets.
Weakening repo-specific HIPAA, plan-mode, LAM, exemption, product-baseline, or trading safety constraints.
research_summary: Issue #459 is the final SSOT v0.2 rollout child. Prior child issues propagated the consumer metadata to LAM and downstream product/exempt repos. The remaining gap is a governance-owned weekly sweep/report surface that summarizes each repo's profile, governance SHA, package version, managed files, overrides, verifier status, workflow pin status, and residual drift while distinguishing critical failures from migration warnings.

sprints.name: Org consumer adoption report
sprints.goal: Produce a deterministic, non-mutating report across governed repos and expose it in the weekly sweep.
tasks: Add a reporter that resolves sweep-enabled governed repos from the registry.
Reuse the existing consumer verifier for per-repo status.
Report profile, governance SHA, package version, managed files, local overrides, verifier status, workflow pin status, and residual drift.
Classify verifier failures as critical failures and warnings as migration warnings.
Emit both Markdown and JSON outputs.
Wire the reporter into overlord-sweep and focused Local CI coverage.
Add tests for passing, missing-record critical, warning-only, and workflow-pin drift cases.
Refine report classification so exact stale workflow SHAs are migration warnings while mutable refs remain critical.
Tighten forbidden override detection so negated guardrail language such as may not weaken is not a false critical.
acceptance_criteria: Weekly governance sweep can report every sweep-enabled consumer repo's profile, governance SHA, package version, managed files, local overrides, verifier status, workflow pin status, and residual drift.
The report distinguishes critical failures from migration warnings.
JSON and Markdown report outputs are deterministic enough for tests and weekly artifacts.
Product repo rollout status is mirrored in governance roadmap/progress surfaces with issue refs.
Epic closeout includes org-level adoption report and residual issue-backed follow-ups.
Stage 6 closeout hook and graph/wiki refresh run before done.

specialist_reviews.focus: Current verifier, sweep workflow, governed repo registry, local CI profile, and closeout protocol.
specialist_reviews.summary: The implementation can remain governance-only by adding a reporter around the existing verifier and wiring it into the scheduled sweep. Downstream remediation remains issue-backed follow-up work.

alternate_model_review.summary: Codex QA will review the Sonnet worker diff, run focused tests, run Local CI Gate, validate the closeout hook, and verify PR checks before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Governance-only implementation of the issue #459 SSOT consumer adoption drift report and closeout artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run supervised Claude Sonnet worker for the bounded implementation slice, then Codex QA/gates and Stage 6 closeout.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Reporter emits per-repo consumer status and separates critical failures from migration warnings.
Focused tests and Local CI Gate pass before merge.
Stage 6 closeout is recorded before issue closure.

material_deviation_rules: If a downstream repo shows drift, record it in the report and route remediation to issue-backed follow-up instead of editing the repo here.
If the supervised Sonnet worker stalls, preserve session evidence and continue with Codex orchestration plus Codex QA.
If Local CI surfaces unrelated pre-existing drift, record it in validation and do not broaden the implementation scope.
approved_by: operator approval: loop remaining child issues, no HITL
